description: 'Bhooth Bangla', directed by Priyadarshan and starring Akshay Kumar, Tabu, Paresh Rawal, Wamiqa Gabbi, Rajpal Yadav, Mithila Palkar and Asrani, is chasing the Rs. 100 crore India net milestone as it enters Day 6 of its theatrical run. With a worldwide gross of Rs. 118.12 crore, the horror-comedy has recovered strongly from its Monday dip with an 18.5% growth on Day 5. The film opened with Rs. 12.25 crore on Day 1, peaked at Rs. 23 crore on Day 3 and is now building toward a healthy first week total. Marking Akshay Kumar and Priyadarshan's reunion after 16 years, the supernatural comedy-thriller follows Arjun Acharya returning to his ancestral home to confront the demonic entity Vadhusur. Stay updated with all live Day 6 tracking, day-wise collections and the complete box office story of Bhooth Bangla.

‘Tactically very good’: Why ex-India cricketer backs Kishan to lead SRH over Cummins
description: Former India coach Sanjay Bangar advocates for Ishan Kishan to retain the Sunrisers Hyderabad captaincy even after Pat Cummins' return. Bangar highlighted Kishan's tactical acumen and game control under pressure, citing his effective use of bowlers, particularly spinners. He believes continuity in leadership will benefit the franchise, especially considering potential fitness concerns for Cummins.

title: 2006 Malegaon blast case: Accused get court nod to challenge charges framed by NIA
description: Bombay High Court has permitted four individuals accused in the 2006 Malegaon blasts to challenge charges under the UAPA and other offenses. The court's decision allows them to contest charges including murder and attempt to murder. This development comes after the framing of 19 charges against them last year, with the NIA investigating the case.

description: Diet Coke is disappearing from shelves in major Indian cities due to a shortage of aluminum cans, exacerbated by global supply disruptions. The beverage's heavy reliance on cans, coupled with surging demand for sugar-free options, makes it particularly vulnerable. Companies are exploring costlier overseas imports as domestic production struggles to meet demand.

description: Virginia voters approved a referendum granting the Democratic-controlled legislature power to redraw congressional maps, potentially shifting the state's representation from a 6-5 Democratic edge to a 10-1 dominance. AI-generated MAGA influencer: Indian student behind 'hot girl' profile with millions of followers
description: An India-based medical student created and operated a popular AI-generated Instagram influencer, "Emily Hart," who amassed millions of followers with patriotic and political content targeting conservative US audiences. The virtual persona, presented as a registered nurse, was monetized through merchandise and subscription platforms, generating thousands of dollars monthly before the account was removed for fraudulent activity.

Odisha school headmaster puts stinging nettle in 10 students’ trousers as punishment
description: A shocking incident unfolded at a government school in Jajpur where a headmaster allegedly inserted stinging nettles into the trousers of ten students for causing classroom disturbances. The students suffered severe itching and rashes. Following widespread condemnation and protests by parents, the headmaster has been suspended pending further action, despite offering an apology.

title: Meta to install tracking software on American employee's machines: Here is what the memo said
description: Meta is installing new tracking software on US employees' computers to capture mouse movements, clicks, and keystrokes for AI training. This initiative aims to improve AI agents' ability to perform work tasks autonomously, like using shortcuts. Meta assures employees the data won't be used for performance reviews, only for enhancing AI models.
